title: anacamptis papilionacea subsp. rubra
intro: Anacamptis papilionacea subsp. rubra, also known as the red helleborine or red marsh orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Anacamptis papilionacea subsp. rubra can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
